articles.title: After Beirut bombed again, Iran warns Israel to 'watch the sky tonight' as fears of retaliation grow
articles.description: Tensions surged after Israel launched fresh strikes on Beirut’s Dahiyeh suburb, prompting a dramatic warning from Iran. Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Rezaei vowed a “decisive and painful” response and urged Israelis to “watch the sky tonight,” fueling fears of imminent retaliation. The threat came after the I.D.F. said it targeted Hezbollah infrastructure following rocket fire from Lebanon. As residents fled parts of Beirut fearing more bombardments, concerns grew that the fragile ceasefire framework could collapse. With attacks continuing across southern Lebanon, the risk of a wider regional confrontation is once again rising sharply.

articles.title: News in Pics | June 8, 2026: Best Photos from around the world
TDP finalises 3 names for Upper House
articles.description: The Telugu Desam Party has selected three candidates for the upcoming Rajya Sabha elections. Sana Sathish Babu will be renominated. Other chosen candidates are educationist Bhashyam Ramakrishna and Vijay Chinthakayala, son of Andhra Pradesh Speaker Chinthakayala Ayyana Patrudu. The NDA alliance secured a majority in the assembly elections, enabling them to claim four Rajya Sabha seats.

articles.title: J&K LG flags off 'Nasha Mukt' padyatra, urges collective action
articles.description: J&K LG Manoj Sinha flagged off the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan padyatra in Poonch, urging collective action against drug abuse. The campaign aims to raise awareness, strengthen preventive measures, and ensure community participation in the fight against addiction.

articles.description: A woman in Thiruvananthapuram files a caste discrimination complaint against a school bus coordinator, leading to police investigation.

articles.title: LPG price hike: TS Singh Deo warns of pressure amid West Asia crisis
articles.description: Congress leader TS Singh Deo linked the domestic LPG price hike to the West Asia crisis, urging the Centre for transparency. The government defended the move, while Karnataka Minister Yathindra Siddaramaiah also criticised the decision.

articles.description: US Sanctions: President Donald Trump states he will not unfreeze Iranian assets or lift economic sanctions until a comprehensive peace deal is finalized.

articles.title: After TMC's Madan Mitra's car vandalised, BJP alleges 'got himself attacked to get security'
articles.description: Protesters allegedly hurled eggs and vandalised Mitra's car, forcing him to leave the area. Meanwhile, West Bengal's ruling BJP on Sunday alleged that the purported attack was "staged by his own men" so that he could claim security cover.

articles.title: India’s baby bust: Why birth rates are falling and why Elon Musk is concerned
articles.description: The decline was highlighted in the 2025 State of World Population Report by the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), which estimated India’s fertility rate at 1.9 births per woman.

articles.title: U.S. Shoots Down Iranian Drones Over Hormuz Strait As Gulf Ceasefire Teeters
articles.description: For the second time in two days, U.S. military forces intercepted and destroyed two Iranian one-way attack drones in the Strait of Hormuz earlier today after they were deemed a threat to international maritime traffic, according to American officials. The incident comes amid heightened tensions in the strategically vital waterway, through which a significant portion of the world's oil and commercial shipping passes each day. U.S. Central Command said American forces acted to protect freedom of navigation and ensure the safety of vessels operating in the region. American Officials emphasized that the drones posed a direct risk to maritime security, prompting an immediate defensive response. No damage to commercial ships or coalition assets was reported following the interception. Iran has not yet commented.

articles.title: 'This Is 'ISRAHELL': Israelis 'REGRET' Attacking Iran; Anti-Netanyahu Revolt In Tel Aviv, Jerusalem
articles.description: Thousands of demonstrators marched through Tel Aviv on Saturday during a weekly protest against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his government, condemning the Israel Defence Forces' actions from Iran to Gaza and demanding fresh elections. The rally comes as Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed nine people including three members of the Lebanese military on Saturday, the Lebanese army and state media said, days after the two sides reached a new ceasefire deal. In Jerusalem, Participants accused Netanyahu of driving conflicts in Gaza, Lebanon and Iran. "He's the centre of this catastrophe," said a protester, Shielev in jerusalem. Watch.

articles.title: Pope Leo tells 1.2 million crowd in Madrid that God stands with the poor
articles.description: Around 1.2 million people lined Madrid's main thoroughfare Paseo de la Castellana toward Cibeles Square as Pope Leo presided over an open-air Mass that was expected to be the largest event during his week-long visit to Spain.

articles.title: Singapore blocks China- linked anti-Indian posts, citing threat to multiracial harmony
articles.description: Authorities order YouTube, Facebook and X to restrict access to 14 posts that allegedly sought to divide communities and undermine the city-state’s multicultural model.

articles.title: Lalu Family Declines Official Security, RJD Workers Take Over Guard Duty At Rabri Residence
articles.description:
In a significant political move, former Bihar Chief Minister Rabri Devi, RJD chief Lalu Prasad Yadav and Leader of Opposition in the Bihar Assembly Tejashwi Yadav have reportedly given up their government-provided security cover.

According to party leaders, security personnel deployed for the protection of the three leaders have been sent back. In their place, Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) workers have taken up positions outside Rabri Devi's official residence, claiming responsibility for the family's security.

RJD Supporters Outside Lalu Residence

Large numbers of RJD supporters were seen stationed outside the residence, many carrying sticks and wearing traditional gamchas tied around their heads. Party workers said they were prepared to guard the premises themselves.

Reacting to the development, RJD state president Mangani Lal Mandal said that security cover is periodically reviewed based on established parameters and can be revised following such assessments.

However, he suggested that the reduction in security should not be carried out in a manner that appears disrespectful. When asked whether RJD legislators and Members of Parliament would also consider giving up their security cover, Mandal said the party would deliberate on the issue in the coming days.

The move comes amid a political row over security arrangements for senior opposition leaders in Bihar.

articles.title: Russia downs 339 Ukrainian drones in 13 hours amid Zelenskyy' s call to end war
articles.description: In a significant escalation of the conflict, Russia boasts of successfully downing 339 Ukrainian drones over 13 regions in a mere 13 hours, even near Moscow, illustrating the fierce drone warfare at play. Tragically, the impact of Russian offensives continues to be felt in Ukraine, as recovery efforts in Zaporizhzhia uncover lost lives.

articles.title: 'Loyal To Government, Than Constitution': Allahabad High Court on UP Police
articles.description: The Allahabad High Court observed that Uttar Pradesh police prioritises loyalty to political powers over constitutional duties. Justice Vinod Diwakar highlighted a culture where officers are rewarded for allegiance rather than merit, leading to misuse of police authority and procedural violations.

articles.title: MNF's secret plan to blow up Pakistan embassy: What Zoramthanga reveals in new memoir

articles.title: ‘Main Street vs The Merger’: Hollywood workers rally against $110 bn Paramount-Warner deal
articles.description: A major media merger faces opposition from industry professionals and politicians. They fear the Paramount Skydance acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery will threaten jobs and cultural output. Regulators are expected to approve the deal, but states are preparing lawsuits. Concerns are high for creative talent and behind-the-scenes workers facing declining employment.

articles.title: US-Iran war LIVE: Pakistan urges end of war a day after fresh exchange of strikes
